memoryArtifacts.headline: BIO
memoryArtifacts.description:
Walter II de Beauchamp (1192/3 – 1236),[1] of Elmley Castle in Worcestershire (12 miles south-east of the City of Worcester), was hereditary Sheriff of Worcestershire.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter de Beauchamp於1192年出生在英國武斯特斯爾時，他的父親Sir Walter Beauchamp42歲，母親Bertha de Braose41歲。他於1212年與Joan de Mortimer在英國武斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他於1217年在英國武斯特斯爾被列為keeper of the royal forest 。他去世於1236年4月14日在英國武斯特斯爾艾爾姆利 卡素享年44歲，埋葬在英國武斯特斯爾伍斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Walter de Beauchamp于1192年出生在英国武斯特斯爾时，他的父亲Sir Walter Beauchamp42岁，母亲Bertha de Braose41岁。他于1212年与Joan de Mortimer在英国武斯特斯爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他于1217年在英国武斯特斯爾被列为keeper of the royal forest 。他去世于1236年4月14日在英国武斯特斯爾艾爾姆利 卡素享年44岁，埋葬在英国武斯特斯爾伍斯特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Walter de Beauchamp was born in 1192, in Worcestershire, England, his father, Sir Walter Beauchamp, was 42 and his mother, Bertha de Braose, was 41. He married Joan de Mortimer in 1212, in Worcestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. In 1217, at the age of 25, his occupation is listed as keeper of the royal forest  in Worcestershire, England. He died on 14 April 1236, in Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 44, and was buried in Worcester, Worcestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Walter de Beauchamp est né en 1192, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Walter Beauchamp, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Bertha de Braose, avait 41 ans. Il a épousé Joan de Mortimer en 1212, à Worcestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. En 1217, à l’âge de 25 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est keeper of the royal forest  à Worcestershire, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 14 avril 1236, à Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Worcester, Worcestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Walter de Beauchamp 1192 in Worcestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Walter Beauchamp 42 und seine Mutter Bertha de Braose 41. Er hat 1212 in Worcestershire, England Joan de Mortimer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. 1217, als er 25 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als keeper of the royal forest  in Worcestershire, England aufgeführt. Er starb am 14. April 1236 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England und wurde in Worcester, Worcestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Walter de Beauchamp è nato il nel 1192 nel Worcestershire, England, il suo padre, Sir Walter Beauchamp, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Bertha de Braose, aveva 41 anni. Lui sposato Joan de Mortimer nel 1212, nel Worcestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Nel 1217, all'età di 25 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come keeper of the royal forest  nel Worcestershire, England. Morì il 14 aprile 1236, nel Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolto a Worcester, Worcestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Walter de Beauchampが1192年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Walter Beauchampは42歳，母Bertha de Braoseは41歳であった。1212年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアでJoan de Mortimerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。1217年，25歳の時，イギリスウオルセステルシュアイアでの職業はkeeper of the royal forest と記録されている。彼は1236年4月14日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアエルムリー カストルで44歳で死去し，イギリスウオルセステルシュアイアウオルセスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Walter de Beauchamp(이)가 1192년 영국 워스스터실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Walter Beauchamp(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Bertha de Braose(은)는 41세였다. 그는 1212년 영국 워스스터실르에서 Joan de Mortimer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 1217년, 25세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 워스스터실르에서 keeper of the royal forest 이었다. 그는 1236년 4월 14일 영국 워스스터실르 엘리 카스틀에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워스스터실르 워스스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Walter de Beauchamp nasceu em 1192, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Walter Beauchamp, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Bertha de Braose, tinha 41 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan de Mortimer em 1212, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Em 1217, com 25 anos, sua profissão aparece como keeper of the royal forest  em Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 14 de abril de 1236, em Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, com 44 anos, e foi sepultado em Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Walter de Beauchamp родился в 1192 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Walter Beauchamp было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Bertha de Braose, 41. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan de Mortimer в 1212 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. В 1217 году, в возрасте 25 лет,  он работал по профессии keeper of the royal forest  в местности ворсестиршир, Англия. Он умер 14 апреля 1236 г. в местности элмли кастл, ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Walter de Beauchamp nació en 1192, en Worcestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Walter Beauchamp, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Bertha de Braose, tenía 41 años. Se casó con Joan de Mortimer en 1212, en Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. En 1217, a la edad de 25 años, su ocupación registrada era keeper of the royal forest  en Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Murió el 14 de abril de 1236, en Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultado en Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra.
